baseName: caps standalone vitest below parent-ledger grants

current.name: gives standalone Vitest the same ledger-governed cap as parent grants

rationale: The standalone cap assertion was deliberately strengthened for the no-queue scheduler: standalone Vitest now receives the same ledger-governed cap as an orchestrated parent rather than the obsolete single-process throttle. The replacement still asserts the base declaration's ledger cap boundary while pinning the updated invariant.

baseName: planLaneConcurrency never raises a lane above the per-process fork cap

current.name: never oversubscribes a grant and queues only when fewer workers than jobs exist

rationale: The replacement checks every planned job allocation against the grant and therefore retains the per-process/no-oversubscription bound under the six-job scheduler.

baseName: planLaneConcurrency keeps instantaneous forks (maxConcurrent*perLane) within the grant

current.name: never oversubscribes a grant and queues only when fewer workers than jobs exist

rationale: The replacement directly sums active job workers and asserts the total never exceeds the ledger grant, preserving the instantaneous-fork invariant for the current heterogeneous lane plan.

baseName: REGRESSION: the single-process throttle (grant=2) collapses lanes to serial

current.name: never oversubscribes a grant and queues only when fewer workers than jobs exist

rationale: The obsolete fixed three-lane reproduction became the low-grant branch of the generalized scheduler test, which still proves constrained grants queue jobs rather than oversubscribing.

baseName: FIX: the fair orchestrator grant runs the 3 lanes concurrently

current.name: allocates eight workers across core, fidelity, three integration shards, and DOM
grants three staggered standalone unit suites eight workers each with no queue

rationale: The original three-lane fairness proof is superseded by the exact six-job topology plus the three-suite 24-core no-queue assertion; together they preserve and strengthen immediate concurrent start.

baseName: reserveVitestLaneBudget grants the FAIR core budget, not the single-process throttle

current.name: gives direct Vitest the same ledger-governed cap as the lane orchestrator
grants three staggered standalone unit suites eight workers each with no queue

rationale: The replacement asserts both the exact eight-worker fair reservation and parity between direct Vitest and the lane orchestrator, excluding the former single-process throttle.

baseName: reserveVitestLaneBudget REUSES a parent grant (preserves run-v2 ledger behavior)

current.name: reuses a parent grant without registering a second ledger entry

rationale: This is a direct semantic rename: the current assertion still proves parent grant reuse and explicitly checks that no duplicate ledger reservation is registered.
